note: Pre-existing, already-documented test failures unrelated to any specific PR. Refreshed by tst-s1 (2026-07-16 baseline triage) -- see artefacts/2026-07-16-baseline-test-triage/triage-report.md for the per-file categorization and artefacts/2026-07-16-baseline-test-triage/decisions.md for the RISK-ACCEPT groupings. Removed since the prior 2026-07-12 snapshot: 5 files confirmed now-passing (check-bri-s3.5-nfr-stripe-keys.js, check-gpa-sc06-source-path-guard.js, check-lab-s3.2-stripe-checkout.js, check-lab-s3.4-stripe-webhook.js, run-gpa-tests.js), plus 2 files this story fixed outright (check-bee1-landing-page.js, check-ilc1-capture-schema.js). check-md-3-adr.js's internal T4 logic was also fixed (verified 9/9 passing standalone with no time limit) but it remains in this baseline: run-all-tests.js's own 120s per-file spawnSync timeout always kills its T4 check (a nested npm test taking several minutes) before it can complete when run as part of the aggregate suite -- see triage-report.md's dedicated AC3 section for the full explanation. Added 2026-07-17: tests/check-p3.5-validate-trace.js, which spawns pwsh to run scripts/validate-trace.ps1 under its own fixed timeout, is a symmetric victim of the exact same mechanism -- check-md-3-adr.js's nested full npm test recursion competes for the same limited runner resources, so whenever that nested run happens to squeeze under its 120s window (flipping check-md-3-adr.js to passing), it is consuming CPU at the moment check-p3.5-validate-trace.js's own pwsh spawn needs it, starving that spawn instead. This explains the exact, repeated check-md-3-adr.js/check-p3.5-validate-trace.js pairing observed across many unrelated PRs (#489, #490, #492) rather than random scattering across different files. Doubling check-p3.5-validate-trace.js's own spawnSync timeouts (15s->30s) did not resolve it, confirming the root cause is resource contention from check-md-3-adr.js's recursion, not check-p3.5-validate-trace.js's own timeout value. Used by scripts/ci-test-regression-check.js to gate CI on new regressions only. A file passing that used to be here is fine (remove it next time this list is refreshed). A NEW failing file not in this list fails CI.
